summary: Drew DeVault provides a progress update on Sway, the i3-compatible Wayland compositor, covering developments since December 2015. The project has shipped versioned releases with window borders, input device configuration, and a maturing swaybar and swaylock. Sway 0.5 has landed in the Arch Linux community repository and is spreading to other distros. DeVault outlines the roadmap toward Sway 1.0, highlighting stacked and tabbed window layouts as the most requested remaining feature. He emphasizes the community-driven nature of the project, noting he is no longer the top contributor by lines of code.
key_points: Sway has shipped versioned releases with significant new features including window borders, input device configuration, and a mature swaybar/swaylock
The project has been packaged for Arch Linux and is spreading to other distributions, signaling growing adoption
The include config command enables per-machine configuration under version control, solving a real dotfiles management problem
Development is community-driven with dozens of contributors, and DeVault is no longer the top author by lines of code
The project is working toward Sway 1.0 with stacked and tabbed window layouts as the most requested remaining feature
money_quotes: We are now shipping versioned releases of sway, which include support for window borders, input device configuration, more new features, and many bug fixes and stability improvements.
I'm also happy to say that Sway 0.5 has landed in the Arch Linux community repository and I'm starting to hear rumors of it landing in other Linux distros as well.
Once again, I'm no longer the author of the most lines of code.
As the maintainer of sway, a lot of what I do is reviewing and merging contributions from others.
All of this is only possible thanks to the hard work of dozens of contributors.
key_insight: Sway's rapid progress toward a viable i3-compatible Wayland compositor is driven by a growing community of contributors, with the maintainer's role shifting from primary coder to reviewer and coordinator.
